title: NIS2 Art.2/3/26/27 — Entity scope, registry and jurisdiction evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.2 receives partial assurance because UC evidence proves the monitorable workflow or telemetry portion while legal approval and filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.2 using NIS2 Art.2/3/26/27 — Entity scope, registry and jurisdiction evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search for NIS2 Art.2/3/26/27 — Entity scope, registry and jurisdiction evidence, dashboard panel, and archived result export in index=audit_evidence for Art.2.

dataSources: `lookup=nis2_entity_scope.csv` (fields `entity_id`, `legal_name`, `sector_code`, `competent_authority`, `nis2_classification`); `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_service` with `sys_id`, `name`, `sys_updated_on`, `operational_status`; `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:entity_register` for formal register confirmations; optional `lookup=nis2_sector_overlay.csv`. Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) for CMDB pulls.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)); Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud core premium.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_entity_scope.csv
| join entity_id type=left [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_service earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(sys_updated_on) as cmdb_last_sync values(operational_status) as op_status by u_legal_entity_id
    | rename u_legal_entity_id as entity_id
]
| join entity_id type=left [
    search index=grc sourcetype=grc:entity_register earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(register_status) as rg_status latest(owner) as rg_owner by entity_ref
    | rename entity_ref as entity_id
]
| eval stale_days=if(isnotnull(cmdb_last_sync), round((now()-strptime(cmdb_last_sync,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/86400), null())
| eval evidence_gap=if(isnull(cmdb_last_sync) OR isnull(rg_status) OR rg_status NOT IN ("confirmed","validated") OR (isnotnull(stale_days) AND stale_days>30),1,0)
| lookup nis2_entity_scope_exceptions entity_id OUTPUT exception_until
| where evidence_gap=1 AND (isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| table entity_id, legal_name, nis2_classification, cmdb_last_sync, rg_status, stale_days
description: Joins the authoritative NIS2 entity scope lookup to ServiceNow CMDB service records and a lightweight GRC register feed so missing identifiers, stale synchronisation past thirty days, or unconfirmed classifications surface before supervision or annual attestations.
value: Legal and entity-management teams keep a defensible, time-stamped trail that the organisations listed as essential or important still match what operations runs day to day, which prevents the wrong subsidiaries from inheriting controls or reporting obligations.
implementation: (1) Populate `nis2_entity_scope.csv` under dual-control. (2) Map `entity_id` to CMDB `u_legal_entity_id`. (3) Ingest `grc:entity_register` with `register_status`. (4) Weekly alert on `evidence_gap=1`. (5) Archive results to `index=audit_evidence`.
visualization: Heatmap of stale entities, table of unmatched CMDB rows, single value count of open gaps.
knownFalsePositives:
• **New subsidiary mid-quarter** — Distinguish: CMDB row exists but GRC confirmation pending backlog. Suppress: `nis2_entity_scope_exceptions.csv` with two-week `exception_until` and ticket pointer.
• **Different identifier scheme in legacy CMDB** — Distinguish: `u_legal_entity_id` blank while `vendor_asset_tag` holds key. Suppress: extend transform to normalise IDs before join; temporary filter `where source_system!="legacy_mainframe"`.
• **Planned CMDB freeze** — Distinguish: Change ticket CHG000 known to pause sync 21 days. Suppress: maintenance lookup referencing `chg_record` and auto-expiry.
• **Test sandbox polluting cmdb index** — Distinguish: `environment=test`. Suppress: add `where environment!="test"` on cmdb subsearch.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability before national transposition, does not file regulator notifications, and does not replace counsel review of entity classification.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.47` output, CMDB correlation rows, GRC register excerpts, and tamper-resistant archive in `index=audit_evidence` tied to clause mapping.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Point `nis2_entity_scope.csv` at a synthetic entity whose CMDB `sys_updated_on` is forty-five days old while register policy requires thirty-day freshness; the search must return `evidence_gap=1` with non-null `stale_days` above the threshold.
controlTest.negativeScenario: For an entity with same-day CMDB synchronisation and `register_status=validated`, the alert pipeline removes the row after the `evidence_gap` eval, yielding no results for that identifier in the scheduled window.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ServiceNow CMDB service modelling with a stable `u_legal_entity_id` field populated for in-scope services; verify via `Settings` > `CMDB` UI.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) installed with modular input hitting `/api/now/table/cmdb_ci_service` (or your mapping table) every 15 minutes.
• CSV lookups `nis2_entity_scope.csv` and optional `nis2_sector_overlay.csv` published to `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/search/lookups/` with transforms entries.
• GRC feed landing in `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:entity_register` — can be CSV ingest via `inputs.conf` `monitor://` if no API yet.
• RBAC: analysts need `cmdb`, `grc`, `audit_evidence` indexes.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
On the heavy forwarder, confirm props extract `sys_updated_on`, `operational_status`, `name`. For GRC, enforce TIME_PREFIX if timestamps are embedded. Document retention: recommend 400 days on `cmdb` for trending. If multiple ServiceNow instances exist, add `splunk_server` or `source` filters. Keep `nis2_entity_scope.csv` aligned to board-approved entity list — version file in git.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Fix strptime format to your locale (`%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` if ISO). Schedule weekly; throttle by `entity_id`.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_scope.csv
| join entity_id type=left [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_service earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(sys_updated_on) as cmdb_last_sync values(operational_status) as op_status by u_legal_entity_id
    | rename u_legal_entity_id as entity_id
]
| join entity_id type=left [
    search index=grc sourcetype=grc:entity_register earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(register_status) as rg_status by entity_ref
    | rename entity_ref as entity_id
]
| eval stale_days=if(isnotnull(cmdb_last_sync), round((now()-strptime(cmdb_last_sync,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/86400), null())
| eval evidence_gap=if(isnull(cmdb_last_sync) OR isnull(rg_status) OR rg_status NOT IN ("confirmed","validated") OR (isnotnull(stale_days) AND stale_days>30),1,0)
```

Pipe through `lookup nis2_entity_scope_exceptions` before alerting.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick three live entities: compare Splunk `cmdb_last_sync` to ServiceNow `sys_updated_on`. Spot-check `register_status` vs counsel spreadsheet. Verify negative case by temporarily lowering threshold to 1 day in a dev search head.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add panel to legal risk dashboard, integrate with JIRA legal project for remediation IDs, export weekly CSV to `index=audit_evidence` via `collect` command with `marker` field for audit ID.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **strptime returns null** — epoch or TZ issue; use `strptime` with `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z` or convert with `strftime` first.
• **Join fan-out duplicates** — multiple CMDB rows share `u_legal_entity_id`; dedup with `| stats latest(cmdb_last_sync) by entity_id` before join.
• **Empty grc index** — intake paused; check `splunkd.log` for `TailReader` errors.
• **Over-alerting after merger** — bulk upload exceptions via REST `/servicesNS/nobody/search/data/lookup-table-files/upload` under CAB control.
grandmaExplanation: We make sure the official list of which companies must follow the rules still matches what the computer inventory says, and that nothing important fell out of date quietly. If something does not line up, we tell the legal owner early.
dataModelAcceleration: Optional acceleration if you map feeds into the Alerts data model; otherwise restrict by index and sourcetype as shown.
